title: Search filter logic inverted since v2.3.1 — default 'Active' filter returns archived tasks and vice versa
problem: Since the v2.3.1 patch update (approximately three days ago), the main search bar returns archived tasks when the default 'Active tasks only' filter is selected, and returns active tasks when the 'Archived only' filter is selected. The task status stored on individual tasks is correct — only the search/filter layer is affected.
root_cause_hypothesis: The v2.3.1 patch likely introduced a boolean or enum inversion in the search query's status filter logic. When the filter requests active tasks, the query is matching archived tasks, and vice versa. This could be a flipped conditional, a swapped enum mapping, or an inverted boolean flag in the search index query builder. The fact that task detail views show the correct status rules out a data corruption issue — the problem is isolated to search query construction or index filtering.
reproduction_steps: Deploy or use an instance running v2.3.1
Ensure there are both active and archived tasks with overlapping keywords (e.g., 'Q2 planning')
Open the main search bar with default filter settings ('Active tasks only')
Search for a keyword that matches both an active and an archived task
Observe that archived tasks appear in results while active tasks are missing
Change the archive filter to 'Archived only' and repeat the search
Observe that active tasks now appear in results — confirming the filter is inverted
environment: TaskFlow v2.3.1. Issue is instance-wide, not account-specific. Confirmed by at least two users on the same instance. Previous version (pre-v2.3.1) worked correctly.
impact: All users on any instance running v2.3.1 are affected. Search is effectively unusable under default settings, forcing users to manually invert filters as a workaround. Users who rely heavily on search (e.g., the reporter with ~150 active tasks) are significantly blocked in daily work.
recommended_fix: Diff the search query/filter logic between v2.3.0 and v2.3.1. Look for changes to the status filter predicate in the search query builder — specifically any boolean inversion, swapped enum values, or negation logic change (e.g., `status == 'archived'` where it should be `status != 'archived'`, or a flipped `is_archived` flag). Fix the inversion and add a regression test. This warrants a hotfix (v2.3.2) given the team-wide impact.
proposed_test_case: Create tasks in both active and archived states. Execute a search with the default 'Active tasks only' filter and assert that only active tasks are returned. Execute a search with the 'Archived only' filter and assert that only archived tasks are returned. This test should run against the search query layer specifically, not just the data model.
information_gaps: Whether the issue affects API-based search or only the web UI search bar
Whether other filters (project, date range, assignee) are also inverted or just the archive status filter
The exact changelog or diff for v2.3.1 (not yet examined by the engineering team)
